text: One mole of any substance contains \( 6.022 \times 10^{23} \) entities. This number is called:
name: Avogadro's Number

text: The number of moles in 44 g of \( \text{CO}_2 \) (molar mass = 44 g/mol) is:
name: Moles Calculation

options: The actual number of atoms in a molecule
The simplest whole number ratio of atoms
explanation: Empirical formula gives the simplest whole number ratio of atoms. Molecular formula = n × (empirical formula).
name: Empirical Formula

text: In the reaction \( 2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \to 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \), 4 g of \( \text{H}_2 \) reacts with how many grams of \( \text{O}_2 \)?
explanation: 4 g \(\text{H}_2\) = 2 mol. From stoichiometry, 2 mol \(\text{H}_2\) needs 1 mol \(\text{O}_2\) = 32 g.
name: Stoichiometry Calculation

text: The percentage composition of oxygen in \( \text{H}_2\text{O} \) is:
name: Percentage Composition

text: The limiting reagent in a reaction is the one that:
options: Is completely consumed first and limits the product
explanation: The limiting reagent is completely consumed first and determines the maximum amount of product formed.
name: Limiting Reagent

options: Moles of solute per kg of solvent
Moles of solute per litre of solution
Mass of solute per litre of solution
explanation: Molarity \( M = \frac{\text{moles of solute}}{\text{volume of solution in litres}} \)
name: Molarity Definition

text: The law of conservation of mass states that in a chemical reaction:
options: Mass of products > mass of reactants
Mass of reactants = mass of products
Mass is always lost as energy
explanation: Lavoisier's Law: Total mass of reactants = Total mass of products. Matter is neither created nor destroyed.
name: Conservation of Mass

text: How many molecules are present in 2 moles of \( \text{O}_2 \)?
name: Number of Molecules

text: The volume occupied by 1 mole of an ideal gas at STP (0°C, 1 atm) is:
explanation: Molar volume of ideal gas at STP = 22.4 L/mol (standard condition: 0°C, 1 atm).
name: Molar Volume at STP

text: Normality is related to molarity by:
name: Normality and Molarity

text: The molecular formula of a compound with empirical formula \( \text{CH}_2 \) and molar mass 42 g/mol is:
explanation: Empirical formula mass of \(\text{CH}_2 = 14\). \( n = 42/14 = 3 \). Molecular formula \( = \text{C}_3\text{H}_6 \).
name: Molecular Formula from Empirical

text: The law of definite proportions (Proust's Law) states:
options: Gases combine in simple whole-number volumes
A compound always has elements in fixed mass ratio
Equal volumes of gases have equal molecules
explanation: Proust's Law: A pure compound always contains elements combined in a fixed definite proportion by mass.
name: Law of Definite Proportions
